89:     upsert_script: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs
90:     fanout_run_script: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs
91:     fanout_merge_script: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs
92:     council_dispatch: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs
93:   prompt_framing: .opencode/skills/sk-prompt-models/SKILL.md
94: 
95: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
96: # AGENT CONFIGURATION (native seat)
97: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
98: agent_config:
99:   agent: deep-context
100:   agent_file: ".opencode/agents/deep-context.md"
101:   # Canonical path. Runtime-specific directories (e.g. .claude/agents/) reference this source.
102:   model: opus
103:   leaf_only: true
104:   read_only_analyzer: true
105:   tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, code_graph_query, code_graph_context]
106:   tool_call_budget: { target: 8, max: 12 }
107:   note: "Native seats are READ-ONLY. They return structured findings to the host; they never write merged state."
108: 
109: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
110: # STATE FILE PATHS
111: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
112: state_paths:
113:   packet_dir: "{artifact_dir}"
114:   config: "{artifact_dir}/deep-context-config.json"
115:   state_log: "{artifact_dir}/deep-context-state.jsonl"
116:   strategy: "{artifact_dir}/deep-context-strategy.md"
117:   registry: "{artifact_dir}/findings-registry.json"
118:   dashboard: "{artifact_dir}/deep-context-dashboard.md"
119:   pause_sentinel: "{artifact_dir}/.deep-context-pause"
120:   lock_file: "{artifact_dir}/.deep-context.lock"
121:   prompt_dir: "{artifact_dir}/prompts"
122:   iteration_dir: "{artifact_dir}/iterations"
123:   iteration_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md"
124:   # Per-seat raw findings for an iteration (one file per seat, host-collected before merge).
125:   seat_dir: "{artifact_dir}/seats"
126:   seat_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/seats/iter-{NNN}/{label}.json"
127:   # Per-iteration structured delta stream (one file per iteration) — complement to the
128:   # canonical state_log append. Must contain at least one record whose type === "iteration".
129:   delta_dir: "{artifact_dir}/deltas"
130:   delta_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/deltas/iter-{NNN}.jsonl"
131:   report_output: "{artifact_dir}/context-report.md"
132:   report_json_output: "{artifact_dir}/context-report.json"
133:   archive_root: "{artifact_archive_root}"
134: 
135: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136: # WORKFLOW
137: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
138: workflow:
139: 
140:   step_resolve_artifact_root:
141:     description: "Resolve the context artifact directory from spec_folder (or standalone run dir)"
142:     note: "Root specs keep context artifacts directly in {spec_folder}/context/. Child phases keep local packet dirs in {spec_folder}/context/{packet}/, and archives stay local at {spec_folder}/context_archive/{packet}/. When no spec folder exists, use a standalone run dir and hand context-report.md to /speckit:plan."
143:     command: "node -e \"const { resolveArtifactRoot } = require('.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs'); console.log(JSON.stringify(resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', 'context')));\""
144:     outputs:
145:       - artifact_root_dir: "Resolved local context owner directory"
146:       - artifact_subfolder: "Local packet subfolder ({ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}), else null"
147:       - artifact_dir: "Resolved context packet directory for this target"
148:       - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local context archive directory for this target"
149: 
150:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
151:   # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
152:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
153:   phase_init:
154:     purpose: Create all state files and seed the frontier for the context session
155:     steps:
156: 
157:       step_preflight_contract:
158:         action: "Validate required setup bindings and executable workflow contract before any file writes"
159:         verify:
160:           - required_values_present: [scope, spec_folder, max_iterations, convergence_threshold, executor_pool]
161:           - spec_folder_is_within: "specs/ or .opencode/specs/ (canonicalize alias roots before validation). A standalone run dir is allowed ONLY when no spec folder was named in the scope or derivable from it; FAIL closed if a standalone target is bound while the scope named or derived a spec folder (auto_mode_contract §1 fallback guard)"
162:           - pool_non_empty: "config.fanout.executors has at least one seat (warn when exactly one — no agreement signal)"
163:           - pool_mode: "config.fanout.mode == 'by-model-shared-scope'"
164:           - unresolved_placeholders: "FAIL if any user-facing template references placeholders not produced by earlier steps"
165:           - tool_contract: "FAIL if executable steps reference tools outside the command allowlist"
166:         on_fail: "Halt with STATUS=FAIL and report the missing binding or unsupported contract reference"
167: 
168:       step_create_directories:
169:         action: "Create spec folder and context packet directories if they don't exist"
170:         command: "mkdir -p {state_paths.prompt_dir} {state_paths.iteration_dir} {state_paths.delta_dir} {state_paths.seat_dir}"
171: 
172:       step_acquire_lock:
173:         action: "Acquire the single-writer advisory lock before session classification"
174:         command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs acquire --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --packet {spec_folder}"
175:         on_acquire: "Capture ownerPid from the JSON output and reuse it for every release below. If acquired=false, fail closed — another deep-context run already holds this packet."
176:         note: "Uses the deep-loop-runtime loop-lock helper (single source of truth: stale-lock reclaim, clean owner-scoped release). macOS/BSD locking is advisory — best-effort single-writer, not a hard mutex across host calls; stale-lock override is confirm-only or explicit recovery-only."
177:         on_halt:
178:           action: "Release the lock before any terminal halt branch returns control"
179:           applies_to: ["step_classify_session.on_invalid_state", "step_classify_session.on_completed_session"]
180:           command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --owner {captured_owner_pid}; failures here are logged but do not block the halt"
181:         on_cancel:
182:           action: "Release the lock on user cancel or pause-sentinel detection before sentinel processing"
183:           command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --owner {captured_owner_pid}; cancel completes regardless of cleanup result"
184:         on_workflow_exit:
185:           action: "Release the lock as the final step of any workflow exit path (success or failure)"
186:           command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock {state_paths.lock_file} --owner {captured_owner_pid}; idempotent — safe to invoke when already released"
187: 
188:       step_classify_session:
189:         action: "Classify the session before writing files"
190:         inspect:
191:           - "{state_paths.config}"
192:           - "{state_paths.state_log}"
193:           - "{state_paths.strategy}"
194:         classify:
195:           fresh: "None of the three state artifacts exist"
196:           resume: "Config, JSONL, and strategy all exist and agree on scope/spec folder"
197:           completed-session: "Resume artifacts exist and config.status == 'complete'"
198:           invalid-state: "Any partial, missing, or contradictory combination"
199:         outputs:
200:           - session_state: "fresh | resume | completed-session | invalid-state"
201:         on_resume:
202:           gate3_classification: write-path
203:           gate3_rationale: "resume produces iteration-NNN.md writes and JSONL state-log appends"
204:           log: "Auto-resume: state artifacts are complete and consistent. Continue the same lineage (sessionId unchanged, generation unchanged, no archive)."
205:           append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"context","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"generation":{generation},"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
206:           skip_to: phase_loop
207:         on_restart:
208:           action: "Archive the current context packet under the local context_archive/ tree and start a new lineage segment with a fresh sessionId and generation+1."
209:           command: "if [ -d {state_paths.packet_dir} ]; then mkdir -p {state_paths.archive_root} && mv {state_paths.packet_dir} {state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}; fi"
210:           set:
211:             lineage_mode: "restart"
212:             parent_session_id: "{prior_session_id}"
213:             generation: "{prior_generation + 1}"
214:           append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"context","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
215:           skip_to: step_create_config
216:         on_completed_session:
217:           log: "Prior session already completed. Do not route directly to synthesis; archive the completed context/ tree or start a fresh packet instead."
218:           halt: true
219:           message: "Completed deep-context packet detected. Archive or replace the existing context/ tree before starting a new session."
220:         on_invalid_state:
221:           halt: true
222:           message: "Existing deep-context state is incomplete or contradictory. Repair or archive the invalid context packet before continuing."
223:         on_fresh:
224:           action: "Proceed with a new session"
225: 
226:       step_seed_frontier:
227:         action: "Seed the SLICE frontier from the scope BEFORE the first sweep (never a whole-repo sweep)"
228:         instructions: |
229:           1. Extract anchors from {scope}: paths, symbols, error strings, domain terms.
230:           2. Expand via the System Code Graph: code_graph_query on 2-5 word concept descriptions →
231:              blast-radius / calls → ranked SLICE nodes.
232:           3. If the code graph is stale or unavailable, fall back to Glob+Grep over the anchors.
233:           4. Rank slices by anchor proximity; cap the initial frontier so each iteration's focus
234:              is a tractable slice set, not the whole repo.
235:         outputs:
236:           - frontier_slices_json: "Ranked SLICE node list (path, symbol, kind, rank), else [] when nothing resolved"
237:           - frontier_source: "code_graph | glob_grep_fallback"
238:         append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"frontier_seeded","mode":"context","source":"{frontier_source}","sliceCount":"{frontier_slice_count}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
239: 
240:       step_create_config:
241:         action: "Create deep-context-config.json from template (carry the resolved pool + thresholds)"
242:         template: .opencode/skills/deep-context/assets/deep_context_config.json
243:         populate:
244:           scope: "{scope}"
245:           specFolder: "{spec_folder}"
246:           loopType: "context"
247:           executionMode: "auto"
248:           maxIterations: "{max_iterations}"
249:           # Semantic: per-iteration new-agreement-eligible-findings floor. See convergence.md.
250:           convergenceThreshold: "{convergence_threshold}"
251:           stuckThreshold: 2
252:           maxToolCallsPerIteration: 12
253:           relevanceGate: "{relevance_gate}"
254:           agreementMin: "{agreement_min}"
255:           fanout.mode: "by-model-shared-scope"
256:           fanout.concurrency: "{concurrency}"
257:           fanout.executors: "{executor_pool}"
258:           report.path: "context/context-report.md"
259:           report.jsonPath: "context/context-report.json"
260:           reducer.enabled: true
261:           reducer.registryFile: "context/findings-registry.json"
262:           reducer.dashboardFile: "context/deep-context-dashboard.md"
263:           createdAt: "{ISO_8601_NOW}"
264:           status: "initialized"
265:           lineage.sessionId: "{AUTO_SESSION_ID}"
266:           lineage.parentSessionId: null
267:           lineage.lineageMode: "new"
268:           lineage.generation: 1
269:           lineage.continuedFromRun: null
270:         output: "{state_paths.config}"
271: 
272:       step_create_state_log:
273:         action: "Initialize JSONL state log with config record"
274:         content: '{"type":"config","scope":"{scope}","loopType":"context","maxIterations":{max_iterations},"convergenceThreshold":{convergence_threshold},"relevanceGate":{relevance_gate},"agreementMin":{agreement_min},"fanout":{"mode":"by-model-shared-scope","concurrency":{concurrency}},"createdAt":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","specFolder":"{spec_folder}"}'
275:         output: "{state_paths.state_log}"
276: 
277:       step_create_strategy:
278:         action: "Create strategy file with the seeded frontier and the executor pool roster"
279:         populate:
280:           scope: "{scope}"
281:           frontier: "Render frontier_slices_json as the initial focus candidates (ranked SLICE list)"
282:           executor_pool: "Render the pool roster: one row per seat (label, kind, model, promptFramework)"
283:           known_context: "None"
284:           known_context_summary: "None"
285:           max_iterations: "{max_iterations}"
286:           convergence_threshold: "{convergence_threshold}"
287:           relevance_gate: "{relevance_gate}"
288:           agreement_min: "{agreement_min}"
289:         output: "{state_paths.strategy}"
290: 
291:       step_create_registry:
292:         action: "Initialize host-owned findings registry (merged, deduped, agreement-scored)"
293:         content: '{"reuseCandidates":[],"integrationPoints":[],"conventions":[],"dependencies":[],"gaps":[],"contradictions":[],"metrics":{"iterationsCompleted":0,"findings":0,"agreementEligible":0,"sliceCoverage":0,"reuseCatalogCoverage":0,"agreementRate":0,"relevanceFloor":0,"dependencyCompleteness":0,"convergenceScore":0}}'
294:         output: "{state_paths.registry}"
295: 
296:       step_enrich_strategy_context:
297:         action: "Ensure Known Context is written only after strategy.md exists"
298:         condition: "prior_context_found"
299:         edit: "{state_paths.strategy}"
300:         note: "Append or replace the Known Context section with prior_context_summary and retained detail from prior_context"
301: 
302:       step_init_complete:
303:         log: "Initialization complete. Config, state, strategy, registry created; frontier seeded ({frontier_slice_count} slices via {frontier_source}); pool = {pool_summary}."
304:         set: { current_iteration: 1, stuck_count: 0, prev_agreement_eligible_count: 0, low_progress_streak: 0 }
305: 
306:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
307:   # PHASE 2: PARALLEL-SWEEP ITERATION LOOP
308:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
309:   phase_loop:
310:     purpose: Per iteration, sweep the shared focus with the WHOLE pool in parallel, then merge + converge
311:     repeat: true
312:     max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
313:     steps:
314: 
315:       step_read_state:
316:         action: "Read current state from JSONL, strategy, and registry"
317:         read:
318:           - "{state_paths.state_log}"
319:           - "{state_paths.strategy}"
320:           - "{state_paths.config}"
321:           - "{state_paths.registry}"
322:         extract:
323:           - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
324:           - current_iteration: "iteration_count + 1"
325:           - current_focus: "Frontier slice set for this iteration from strategy.md 'Next Focus', else the seeded frontier"
326:           - prev_agreement_eligible_count: "agreementEligible from the previous iteration record, else 0"
327:           - new_agreement_eligible_last: "newAgreementEligible from the latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
328:           - slice_coverage_latest: "sliceCoverage from latest iteration record, else 0"
329:           - reuse_catalog_coverage_latest: "reuseCatalogCoverage from latest iteration record, else 0"
330:           - agreement_rate_latest: "agreementRate from latest iteration record, else 0"
331:           - relevance_floor_latest: "relevanceFloor from latest iteration record, else 0"
332:           - uncovered_slices: "SLICE nodes without an outgoing COVERED_BY edge (recovery focus candidates)"
333:           - last_3_summaries: "Summarize the latest 3 iteration records as 'iter N: focus (newAgr, agreementRate)'"
334:           - latest_status: "Extract status from latest iteration record, else 'none yet'"
335: 
336:       step_check_pause_sentinel:
337:         action: "Check for pause sentinel file"
338:         check: "Does {state_paths.pause_sentinel} exist?"
339:         if_exists:
340:           log_event: '{"type":"event","event":"userPaused","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"userPaused","sentinelPath":"{state_paths.pause_sentinel}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
341:           message: "Context loop paused. Delete context/.deep-context-pause to resume."
342:           halt: true
343:         if_not_exists:
344:           proceed: true
345: 
346:       # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
347:       # THE PARALLEL HETEROGENEOUS SWEEP
348:       # All seats analyze the SAME current_focus. native batch ‖ CLI pool
349:       # start TOGETHER and barrier-join. Seats are READ-ONLY; they write
350:       # only their own seats/iter-NNN/{label}.json. The host merges after.
351:       # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
352:       step_render_seat_prompts:
353:         action: "Render ONE prompt per seat for the SHARED current focus, carrying the full lineage contract"
354:         ensure_dirs: "mkdir -p {state_paths.prompt_dir}/iter-{current_iteration} {state_paths.seat_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}"
355:         for_each: "seat in config.fanout.executors"
356:         contract_per_seat: |
357:           Every seat prompt MUST carry the four-part lineage contract (a seat told only "analyze"
358:           returns generic noise):
359:             1. gather-subject: "{scope}"
360:             2. scope/slice: the SHARED current_focus (this iteration's SLICE set) — identical for ALL seats
361:             3. known-context: prior_context_summary + already-confirmed reuse candidates (avoid re-finding)
362:             4. output schema: the structured-finding JSON the host expects (see seat_output_schema below)
363:         prompt_framing:
364:           rule: "Apply each seat's promptFramework via sk-prompt-models (MiMo→COSTAR, MiniMax/DeepSeek→TIDD-EC). Native seats use no framework wrapper."
365:           loader: ".opencode/skills/sk-prompt-models/SKILL.md"
366:         seat_output_schema: |
367:           { "findings": [ { "unit_id": "sha256(path:symbol:kind)", "path": "...", "symbol": "...",
368:             "kind": "reuse_candidate|integration_point|convention|dependency|gap",
369:             "signature": "...", "reuse": "extend|compose|wrap|import", "evidence": "file:line",
370:             "relevance": 0.0, "notes": "..." } ] }
371:         output_path: "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}/{seat.label}.md"
372: 
373:       step_parallel_sweep:
374:         action: "Dispatch the WHOLE pool over the SHARED current focus CONCURRENTLY; barrier-join"
375:         note: |
376:           This is the defining step. The native batch AND the CLI pool start TOGETHER (true
377:           heterogeneous parallelism: 2 native + N CLI all at once) and the host waits for ALL
378:           seats before merging. Both sub-steps below are launched WITHOUT waiting for each other.
379:         launch_together: [step_sweep_native_batch, step_sweep_cli_pool]
380:         barrier_join: "Wait for BOTH step_sweep_native_batch AND step_sweep_cli_pool to settle before step_merge_findings"
381:         sub_steps:
382: 
383:           step_sweep_native_batch:
384:             description: "Dispatch ALL native seats as a SINGLE PARALLEL BATCH (one parallel batch, NOT a sequential for_each)"
385:             skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"
386:             dispatch_mode: parallel_batch
387:             critical: |
388:               NATIVE-PARALLEL REQUIREMENT: emit ALL native @deep-context Task dispatches in ONE batch
389:               (a single set of concurrent Task calls), NOT one-at-a-time in a loop. Each native seat
390:               gets the SAME current_focus and its own rendered prompt. Read-only analyzers.
391:             batch:
392:               for_each_native_seat: "seat in native entries of config.fanout.executors"
393:               dispatch:
394:                 agent: deep-context
395:                 model: opus
396:                 read_only: true
397:                 context_source: "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}/{seat.label}.md"
398:                 returns: "structured findings (seat_output_schema)"
399:                 host_writes: "Host writes each seat's return to {state_paths.seat_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}/{seat.label}.json"
400:             wait_for_completion: true
401: 
402:           step_sweep_cli_pool:
403:             description: "Dispatch ALL CLI seats as ONE-SHOT analysis seats over the SHARED focus, in parallel"
404:             skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
405:             dispatch_mode: one_shot_analysis_council
406:             critical: |
407:               ONE-SHOT-ANALYSIS REQUIREMENT (by-model shared scope): each CLI seat performs exactly ONE
408:               read-only analysis pass over the SHARED current_focus this iteration — identical to a native
409:               seat, just a different model lens. A CLI seat does NOT run its own loop. Use the council
410:               parallel scaffold, NOT the autonomous-lineage runner, for the per-iteration sweep.
411:             primary:
412:               tool: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs#dispatchCouncilSeats"
413:               note: |
414:                 Orchestrate the CLI seats from the Node host via the council scaffold:
415:                   dispatchCouncilSeats({ roundId, seats, dispatchSeat, context })
416:                 fans the seats out with Promise.all and aggregates per-seat results (fulfilled/rejected +
417:                 duration). roundId = "iter-{current_iteration}". seats = config.fanout.executors filtered to
418:                 kind != 'native', each carrying its rendered prompt path and the SHARED current_focus.
419:               dispatch_seat: |
420:                 The injected per-seat dispatch function issues a SINGLE one-shot read-only CLI analysis
421:                 call — one `opencode run` / `codex exec` / `claude -p` / `devin --print` per seat — NOT a
422:                 loop. Build each invocation's per-kind flag shape by reusing
423:                 scripts/fanout-run.cjs#buildLineageCommand(lineage, prompt, sandbox, permission); the
424:                 difference is the PROMPT (one-shot analysis contract below, NOT buildLoopPrompt's
425:                 "run phase_init/phase_main_loop/phase_synthesis" text). The host then writes each seat's
426:                 returned findings JSON to {state_paths.seat_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}/{seat.label}.json.
427:               one_shot_prompt_contract: |
428:                 Each seat's one-shot prompt carries the four-part lineage contract from
429:                 step_render_seat_prompts (gather-subject {scope} + SHARED current_focus + known-context +
430:                 seat_output_schema) and instructs the seat to return ONLY the structured-finding JSON for
431:                 this single pass. Apply the seat's promptFramework via sk-prompt-models
432:                 (MiMo→COSTAR, MiniMax/DeepSeek→TIDD-EC). Source the prompt body from the per-seat file
433:                 rendered at {state_paths.prompt_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}/{seat.label}.md.
434:             optional_autonomous_lineage_mode:
435:               when: "operator opt-in only (config.fanout.cliMode == 'autonomous-lineage'); NEVER the default per-iteration path"
436:               tool: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs"
437:               command: |
438:                 node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
439:                   --spec-folder {spec_folder} \
440:                   --loop-type context \
441:                   --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_cli_json}' \
442:                   --base-artifact-dir {state_paths.seat_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}
443:               working_directory: "{repo_root}"
444:               note: |
445:                 fanout-run.cjs spawns AUTONOMOUS FULL-LOOP lineages (buildLoopPrompt tells each subprocess
446:                 to run phase_init/phase_main_loop/phase_synthesis to convergence). That is the
447:                 research/review autonomous-lineage model — it is NOT by-model shared scope and must NOT be
448:                 used for the per-iteration parallel sweep. Reserve it for an explicit operator opt-in where
449:                 each CLI executor should run its OWN context loop end-to-end and the host merges the
450:                 lineage reports afterward (via scripts/fanout-merge.cjs), not per-iteration findings.
451:             cli_contract:
452:               - "cli-opencode seats: dispatched with closed stdin (</dev/null) and NO top-level --agent (current opencode rejects --agent general); model id form per cli-opencode SKILL.md."
453:               - "cli-codex seats: codex exec --model X -c model_reasoning_effort -c approval_policy=never --sandbox read-only (read-only analyzer)."
454:               - "cli-claude-code seats: claude -p ... --model X --permission-mode plan (read-only)."
455:               - "cli-devin seats: devin --print --prompt-file ... --model X --permission-mode auto (read-only analyzer; --agent-config pins a read-only allowlist)."
456:               - "Self-invocation guards: cli-opencode/cli-devin branches must only run from a non-self dispatch surface; the executor skill SKILL.md §SELF-INVOCATION PROHIBITED contract is authoritative."
457:               - "Executor-audit recursion guard: spawn each CLI seat with the deep-loop-runtime executor-audit dispatch env (SPECKIT_CLI_DISPATCH_STACK appended with this loop's kind via buildExecutorDispatchEnv) so a seat cannot recursively launch another deep-context loop; record seat provenance per the runtime executor-audit contract."
458:             wait_for_completion: true
459: 
460:       step_collect_seat_findings:
461:         action: "Collect every seat's structured findings; record per-seat dispatch outcome"
462:         read_pattern: "{state_paths.seat_dir}/iter-{current_iteration}/*.json"
463:         extract:
464:           - seat_findings_by_label: "Map of {label} -> findings[] (parse each seat file)"
465:           - seats_succeeded: "Labels whose seat file parsed with >=0 findings"
466:           - seats_failed: "Labels with missing/empty/unparseable seat files (timeout, auth, crash)"
467:         note: "Graceful degradation: a failed seat does not abort the sweep; the merge proceeds with surviving seats and agreement degrades."
468:         append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"sweep_settled","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"focusSliceCount":"{current_focus_slice_count}","seatsSucceeded":{seats_succeeded_json},"seatsFailed":{seats_failed_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
469:         on_all_seats_failed:
470:           set: { empty_sweep_streak: "{empty_sweep_streak + 1}" }
471:           log: "All seats failed this sweep ({empty_sweep_streak} consecutive)."
472:           if_streak_at_3: { skip_to: phase_synthesis, set: { reason: "error" } }
473: 
474:       step_merge_findings:
475:         action: "HOST merge: dedup by unit_id, union attribution, set agreement, apply relevance gate, surface contradictions"
476:         algorithm: |
477:           1. unit_id = sha256(path:symbol:kind) for each finding (matches coverage-graph node id).
478:           2. Dedup: group all findings (across ALL surviving seats) by unit_id.
479:           3. Union per-executor attribution: producedBy = distinct seat labels that emitted the unit.
480:           4. agreement = count(distinct executors in producedBy)  → also a metadata.confirmations value
481:              and CONFIRMS edges in the coverage-graph.
482:           5. Relevance gate: drop units whose max(relevance across producers) < config.relevanceGate (0.55).
483:              Keep near-misses (relevance in [0.40, 0.55)) tagged `marginal` for the report's Gaps section.
484:           6. agreement-eligible: a unit with agreement >= config.agreementMin (default 2).
485:           7. Contradictions: when two seats assert incompatible contracts for the same unit_id
486:              (e.g. different signature/reuse verb), record a CONTRADICTS pair; NEVER silently resolve.
487:           8. Verify each surviving unit's file:symbol against the code graph; label verified|unverified.
488:           9. Reuse the fanout-merge.cjs attribution shape so registry/report stay consistent with the
489:              runtime's merge output.
490:         runtime_helper:
491:           tool: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs"
492:           note: "fanout-merge.cjs provides the canonical per-executor attribution/union shape for merged findings; reuse it (or its shape) so the registry matches the runtime contract."
493:         outputs:
494:           - merged_findings_json: "Deduped, attributed, relevance-gated findings with agreement counts"
495:           - new_agreement_eligible_count: "Count of agreement-eligible findings NEW this iteration (not in prior registry)"
496:           - contradictions_json: "Array of CONTRADICTS pairs surfaced this iteration"
497:           - graph_nodes_json: "Coverage-graph nodes for merged units (kinds: SLICE, FILE, SYMBOL, PATTERN, REUSE_CANDIDATE, DEPENDENCY, CONSTRAINT, GAP)"
498:           - graph_edges_json: "Coverage-graph edges (COVERED_BY for swept slices, CONFIRMS per agreement, CONTRADICTS, DEPENDS_ON/IMPORTS, REUSES)"
499: 
500:       step_write_iteration:
501:         action: "HOST writes the iteration narrative + canonical JSONL iteration record + delta file"
502:         write_iteration_file:
503:           path: "{state_paths.iteration_pattern}"
504:           content: "Iteration {current_iteration} narrative: focus swept, per-seat contribution, merged findings with agreement, contradictions, slice coverage delta."
505:         append_iteration_jsonl:
506:           target: "{state_paths.state_log}"
507:           record: '{"type":"iteration","run":{current_iteration},"mode":"context","status":"evidence","focus":"{current_focus_label}","findingsCount":{merged_findings_count},"newAgreementEligible":{new_agreement_eligible_count},"agreementEligible":{cumulative_agreement_eligible_count},"sliceCoverage":{slice_coverage},"reuseCatalogCoverage":{reuse_catalog_coverage},"agreementRate":{agreement_rate},"relevanceFloor":{relevance_floor},"dependencyCompleteness":{dependency_completeness},"contradictions":{contradictions_count},"seatsSucceeded":{seats_succeeded_count},"durationMs":{sweep_duration_ms},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
508:         append_delta:
509:           path: "{state_paths.delta_pattern}"
510:           note: "Must contain at least one record whose type === 'iteration' (host-written) so state can be rehydrated after a partial run."
511: 
512:       step_graph_upsert:
513:         action: "Persist this iteration's coverage-graph nodes/edges (loop_type=context)"
514:         if_graph_events_present:
515:           bash: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --spec-folder \"{spec_folder}\" --loop-type \"context\" --session-id \"{config.lineage.sessionId}\" --nodes '{graph_nodes_json}' --edges '{graph_edges_json}'"
516:           note: "Node kinds + relations for loop_type=context are defined in coverage-graph-db.ts (VALID_KINDS.context / VALID_RELATIONS.context). Skip silently when there are no graph events."
517:         if_graph_events_missing:
518:           proceed: true
519: 
520:       step_update_registry:
521:         action: "Refresh the merged findings registry + dashboard deterministically from iteration state"
522:         bash: "node .opencode/skills/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}"
523:         note: "The reducer is the writer (parity with deep-research/deep-review): re-reads deep-context-state.jsonl + every seat finding, repairs a crash-truncated JSONL tail before read, validates each seat finding before merge, and writes findings-registry.json + dashboard via atomic temp+rename. This is the step that auto-utilizes the runtime robustness; it surfaces stateLogRepair + seatValidationWarnings in its JSON result."
524:         outputs:
525:           - registry_path: "{state_paths.registry}"
526:           - dashboard_path: "{state_paths.dashboard}"
527: 
528:       step_graph_convergence:
529:         action: "Evaluate coverage-graph convergence and persist the decision snapshot"
530:         bash: 'node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs --spec-folder "{spec_folder}" --loop-type "context" --session-id "{config.lineage.sessionId}" --iteration {current_iteration}'
531:         note: "convergence.cjs#evaluateContext returns CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED from the context signals (sliceCoverage, reuseCatalogCoverage, agreementRate, relevanceFloor, dependencyCompleteness) plus contradiction warnings. See convergence.md §3."
532:         outputs:
533:           - graph_decision: "CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED, else null when unavailable"
534:           - graph_decision_json: "JSON-encoded graph decision string, else null"
535:           - graph_signals_json: "Tool signals object, else {}"
536:           - graph_blockers_json: "Tool blockers array, else []"
537:           - graph_convergence_score: "signals.blendedScore when present, else best available graph score, else 0"
538:         append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"decision":{graph_decision_json},"signals":{graph_signals_json},"blockers":{graph_blockers_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
539: 
540:       step_check_convergence:
541:         action: "Combine the host per-iteration saturation check with the graph context decision"
542:         algorithm: |
543:           1. if iteration_count >= max_iterations: STOP (maxIterationsReached). Terminal cap; record
544:              failed guards/blockers as synthesis evidence instead of continuing.
545:           2. if stuck_count >= config.stuckThreshold: STUCK_RECOVERY.
546:           3. HOST SATURATION CHECK (per-iteration new agreement-eligible findings):
547:                new_ratio = new_agreement_eligible_count / max(1, merged_findings_count)
548:                if new_ratio < convergence_threshold: low_progress_streak += 1, else low_progress_streak = 0
549:                host_saturated = low_progress_streak >= 2   (K=2 successive low-progress iterations)
550:           4. COMBINED STOP RULE (agreement + relevance are blocking guards, enforced by the graph):
551:                - STOP only if host_saturated AND graph_decision == "STOP_ALLOWED" (or absent).
552:                - if host_saturated AND graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED":
553:                    decision = "BLOCKED"; reason = "blockedStop"
554:                    blocked_by = graph_blockers (e.g. sliceCoverage<0.70, relevanceFloor<0.50, agreementRate<0.50)
555:                    recovery_strategy = first blocker message, else "Sweep uncovered slices; raise agreement before stopping."
556:                - else if host_saturated AND STOP_ALLOWED: decision = "STOP"; reason = "converged"
557:                - else: decision = "CONTINUE"
558:           See convergence.md for the full signal table, composite weights, and stop contract.
559:         outputs:
560:           - decision: "STOP | BLOCKED | STUCK_RECOVERY | CONTINUE"
561:           - reason: "converged | maxIterationsReached | blockedStop | stuckRecovery | error"
562:           - blocked_by_json: "JSON array of graph blocker names when decision == BLOCKED"
563:           - blocked_by_csv: "Comma-separated graph blocker names when decision == BLOCKED"
564:           - recovery_strategy: "One-line hint for the next focus when BLOCKED or STUCK_RECOVERY"
565: 
566:       step_emit_blocked_stop:
567:         action: "Persist a first-class blocked_stop event when convergence returns blocked, then continue with a recovery focus"
568:         if_blocked:
569:           append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"blocked_stop","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"blockedBy":{blocked_by_json},"signals":{graph_signals_json},"recoveryStrategy":"{recovery_strategy}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
570:           log: "Graph convergence blocked STOP. Failed blockers: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery: {recovery_strategy}"
571:           set:
572:             decision: "CONTINUE"
573:             reason: "blockedStop"
574:             next_focus: "RECOVERY: {recovery_strategy}. Prioritize uncovered slices: {uncovered_slices}"
575: 
576:       step_handle_convergence:
577:         action: "Act on convergence decision"
578:         if_stop:
579:           log: "Loop stopping. Reason: {reason}"
580:           skip_to: phase_synthesis
581:         if_stuck_recovery:
582:           append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"stuckRecovery","mode":"context","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"stuckRecovery","fromIteration":{current_iteration},"outcome":"recovered","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
583:           log: "Stuck detected ({stuck_count} consecutive low-progress sweeps). Widening focus."
584:           set: { next_focus: "RECOVERY: Widen the slice frontier. Uncovered slices: {uncovered_slices}" }
585:           reset: { stuck_count: 0 }
586:         if_continue:
587:           log: "Iteration {current_iteration} complete. New agreement-eligible: {new_agreement_eligible_count}. Next focus: {next_focus}"
588:           set: { next_focus: "Advance the frontier: prioritize uncovered/low-agreement slices for the next shared sweep" }
589: 
590:       step_normalize_lifecycle_events:
591:         action: "Normalize pause and recovery lifecycle events to the frozen STOP_REASONS enum at emission time"
592:         stop_reasons_enum: [converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused]
593: 
594:       step_update_tracking:
595:         action: "Update loop tracking variables"
596:         set:
597:           current_iteration: "{current_iteration + 1}"
598:           stuck_count: "if new_agreement_eligible_count == 0 and merged_findings_count > 0: stuck_count + 1, else 0"
599:         log: "Iteration {N} complete. New agreement-eligible: {new_agreement_eligible_count}. sliceCoverage: {slice_coverage}. Stuck count: {stuck_count}."
600: 
601:       step_loop_decision:
602:         action: "Return to step_read_state for the next iteration (next parallel sweep)"
603:         goto: step_read_state
604: 
605:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
606:   # PHASE 3: SYNTHESIS
607:   # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
608:   phase_synthesis:
609:     purpose: Compile the merged findings into the reuse-first Context Report
610:     steps:
611: 
612:       step_hydrate_summary_metrics:
613:         action: "Hydrate summary metrics before synthesis"
614:         read:
615:           - "{state_paths.config}"
616:           - "{state_paths.state_log}"
617:           - "{state_paths.registry}"
618:         extract:
619:           - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
620:           - executor_pool_summary: "Pool roster from config (e.g. native×2; or native + cli seats for a custom pool)"
621:           - slice_coverage_final: "sliceCoverage from latest iteration record"
622:           - reuse_catalog_coverage_final: "reuseCatalogCoverage from latest iteration record"
623:           - agreement_rate_final: "agreementRate from latest iteration record"
624:           - relevance_floor_final: "relevanceFloor from latest iteration record"
625:           - dependency_completeness_final: "dependencyCompleteness from latest iteration record"
626:           - reason: "Latest stop reason if present, else 'converged' (member of STOP_REASONS enum)"
627: 
628:       step_read_registry_and_iterations:
629:         action: "Read the merged registry and all iteration files"
630:         read:
631:           - "{state_paths.registry}"
632:         read_pattern: "{state_paths.iteration_dir}/iteration-*.md"
633: 
634:       step_verify_citations:
635:         action: "Verify every cited file:symbol against the code graph before it enters the report"
636:         instructions: |
637:           For each reuse candidate / integration point / convention citation, confirm the file:symbol
638:           still resolves in the code graph (code_graph_query). Label verified | unverified.
639:           A stale reference is worse than omission — never ship an unlabeled stale citation.
640: 
641:       step_compile_report:
642:         action: "Compile the reuse-first Context Report (pointers, not source bodies)"
643:         template: .opencode/skills/deep-context/assets/context_report_template.md
644:         sections:
645:           - "Header: scope, executor pool, iteration count, stop reason"
646:           - "1. REUSE Catalog (highest value): id, symbol (file:line), signature, reuse verb, confidence, agreement (k/N), freshness, notes"
647:           - "2. Integration Points: [HARD]/[soft] entry/exit symbols with file:line"
648:           - "3. Touch List: files to change in implementation order"
649:           - "4. Conventions: file:line exemplars (naming, error handling, logging, test layout)"
650:           - "5. Pruned Dependency Subgraph: only edges within the touch radius"
651:           - "6. Prior Art / Decisions: related specs/ADRs/memory, date-stamped"
652:           - "7. Gaps & Unknowns: not-found/unverified + marginal-relevance near-misses"
653:           - "Methodology & Confidence: per-executor attribution, agreement counts, relevance gate, contradictions surfaced"
654:         output: "{state_paths.report_output}"
655: 
656:       step_emit_report_json:
657:         action: "Emit the machine-readable Context Report companion for downstream consumers (/speckit:plan, /speckit:implement)"
658:         instructions: |
659:           Write the same merged findings as structured JSON: reuseCandidates[], integrationPoints[],
660:           touchList[], conventions[], dependencySubgraph{}, priorArt[], gaps[], plus per-finding
661:           attribution {producedBy[], agreement, relevance, freshness} and the convergence signals.
662:         output: "{state_paths.report_json_output}"
663: 
664:       step_convergence_report:
665:         action: "Generate and append the convergence report to the Context Report"
666:         report: |
667:           ## Convergence Report
668:           - Stop reason: {reason}
669:           - Total iterations (parallel sweeps): {iteration_count}
670:           - Executor pool: {executor_pool_summary}
671:           - Final signals: sliceCoverage={slice_coverage_final}, reuseCatalogCoverage={reuse_catalog_coverage_final}, agreementRate={agreement_rate_final}, relevanceFloor={relevance_floor_final}, dependencyCompleteness={dependency_completeness_final}
672:           - Relevance gate: {relevance_gate} · Agreement min: {agreement_min} executors
673:           - Convergence threshold: {convergence_threshold}
674:         append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"synthesis_complete","mode":"context","totalIterations":{iteration_count},"stopReason":"{reason}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
675: 
676:       step_update_config_status:
677:         action: "Mark config as complete"
678:         edit: "{state_paths.config}"

(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-678. Use offset=679 to continue.)
